opp.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Important' and the Merlin product line shows Merlin plus Merlin Intake, but the supporting fields - agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary, AI-RFP rating - are all empty. There is no rep-captured buyer commentary backing the 'Important' tag.
• The Feb 18 discovery call with Mike Bailey (23 minutes, short for a qualifying conversation) yielded only logistics action items: recap today's discussion, summarize via email, and propose dates for a 30-minute follow-up. No buyer mentions of AI, Merlin, intake or agentic surfaced in captured content.
• The Feb 24 and Apr 7 meetings were cancelled or not recorded, and the May 7 session is also unrecorded. With only one short qualification call on record and no buyer-voiced AI signal, the 'Important' tag is doing the work here, which is enough for Curious but not enough for confidence.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb 2026, Mike Bailey
Next Step series (Mar–Jun) reflects continued exploratory engagement but no business-case discussion
metrics.narrative: Mike Bailey articulated operational pain on 18 Feb: decentralized purchasing across five locations (MN, IL, GA, OR, WA) creates limited visibility and process inefficiency. He stated their crude PO system 'doesn't function very efficiently' and only he and Matt use it, describing the purchase-to-invoice flow as 'pretty disjointed.' No quantified business case (savings target, ROI, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement) has been surfaced. Mike downplayed maverick spend ('I don't think it's a tremendous amount') and noted interest in centralization and visibility but did not tie it to a financial metric. The pain is confirmed and operational; quantifying the value case is the gap and must be a priority move.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey passive, exploratory tone, no advocacy signals
Next Step series Mar–Jun: Mike Bailey is sole contact, no escalation or multi-threading
Opportunity Contact Roles: only Mike Bailey and Matt Toso, both operational
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Mike Bailey is the primary contact but has demonstrated no advocacy, no senior access, and no proactive deal progression. He is a coordinator in an exploratory phase, not a driver. On 18 Feb he stated he received an email from Zycus ('otherwise I'd know nothing about Zycus') and is 'just doing due diligence.' He expressed cost concerns ('I don't know if they cost effectively') and deferred to the ERP decision and consulting company recommendations. Mike has not introduced Zycus to senior stakeholders, has not pushed the evaluation forward, and has postponed discovery four times. There is no evidence of active sponsorship or internal advocacy. This is a weak, passive contact relationship, not a champion.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey named Zip as recent vendor engagement
Competitors__c field unknown (not read in Q3 SOQL for this deal)
competition.narrative: Zip is the named competitor. Mike Bailey stated on 18 Feb that he and Matt 'had a conversation with Zip a few weeks ago' and that Zycus 'looked kinda similar maybe,' confirming Zip is in active consideration. No other competitors named. Mike also noted concern that the consulting company involved in the ERP evaluation might recommend another P2P solution, indicating potential for additional competition to surface post-ERP decision. Zip is a direct peer threat in the P2P space. Zycus has not yet differentiated or run a competitive positioning discussion.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey, Matt Toso
identify_pain.narrative: Mike Bailey clearly articulated operational pain on 18 Feb: decentralized purchasing across multiple locations (MN, IL, GA, OR, WA) with limited visibility, a crude and underutilized PO system ('Matt and I are about the only ones using it'), and a disjointed paper flow from purchase through packing slips to invoices. He described the process as 'pretty disjointed' and noted they are looking to 'centralize as much of that as possible' over the next few years. Matt Toso added that communication is fragmented across email, Microsoft Teams, and Monday, and expressed frustration ('no one knows how to communicate anymore'). Mike is the pain owner for procurement process inefficiency. The pain is operational and process-driven, tied to lack of adoption and visibility, not financial distress.

sources: No Salesforce fields or Avoma evidence address paper process
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, InfoSec, or MSA discussion has occurred. The deal has not progressed beyond exploratory intro and postponed discovery calls. No mention of approval requirements, legal review, signature authority, or contract timeline. Given the ERP evaluation is still unresolved and discovery has not occurred in depth, paper process is entirely unknown. This is expected at this stage but becomes a critical path risk with 31 days to close.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey and Matt Toso only
Opportunity Contact Roles: Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager), Matt Toso (no role specified)
Account contact list includes CFO contact but no opp-level engagement recorded
Next Step history (Mar–Jun): no mention of budget, EB, or senior stakeholder involvement
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. All five touchpoints (18 Feb, 24 Feb cancelled, 7 Apr, 7 May, 11 Jun) involve only Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager) and Matt Toso (no title on record). Mike confirmed on 18 Feb he and Matt 'do some of the purchasing' but acknowledged they are operational contacts in an exploratory phase. A $120k investment requires CFO or senior finance sign-off. The account contact list includes a CFO contact, but no engagement on this opportunity is recorded. No budget discussion, approval authority, or senior sponsor has surfaced across any call or Next Step entry. This is a critical gap 31 days from the forecast close date.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey described ERP evaluation in progress, consultant involved, serial process
Next Step 24 Mar, 7 Apr, 12 May, 26 May, 2 Jun, 22 Jun, 29 Jun: discovery postponed four times, explicitly tied to ERP decision
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a serial evaluation: ERP selection first, then P2P tooling. Mike Bailey noted on 18 Feb they are 'in the middle of selecting a new ERP system' and working with a consulting company, and expressed concern about not knowing what the ERP would provide or whether the consultant might recommend another P2P solution. Next Step entries from March through June show discovery repeatedly postponed pending ERP finalization. The ERP evaluation involved full-day meetings with vendors (week of 24 Feb per Mike's comment on 18 Feb: 'Monday and Tuesday next week'). No approval chain, procurement process, or decision timeline for P2P has been articulated. The decision process is buyer-dependent and externally paced (consultant-led ERP selection), with Zycus in a holding pattern.

sources: Avoma intro call 18 Feb: Mike Bailey stated module interest and pain points
Next Step 22 Jun, 29 Jun: ERP finalists narrowed to Netsuite and Agriware; P2P evaluation contingent on ERP decision
decision_criteria.narrative: Mike Bailey stated interest in three modules on 18 Feb: eProcurement ('what brought us together'), sourcing ('might be some value'), and spend analysis. He explicitly ruled out contract management ('we don't do a lot of contracts') and was uncertain on eInvoicing. The buyer's evaluation criteria center on centralizing decentralized purchasing, improving visibility, and streamlining the purchase-to-invoice process. However, no formal RFP, scorecard, or structured evaluation process has been discussed. The ERP selection (Netsuite vs Agriware) is the primary decision in flight, and Mike noted on 18 Feb that Zycus evaluation timing depends on ERP finalization. Criteria are inferred from pain articulation but not formalized.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score on record. Mike Bailey showed interest in the Merlin intake conversational AI on 18 Feb ('You said that, it'll read an email and turn it into a purchase order?'), and the fragmented communication pain (Teams, Monday, email) plus low adoption of their crude PO system align well with AI-driven intake. However, no deep AI appetite or strategic AI initiative has been articulated. Buyer is AI Curious, not AI Hungry.

business_case.evidence: Mike Bailey articulated qualitative pain on 18 Feb: decentralized purchasing, limited visibility, disjointed purchase-to-invoice process, underutilized PO system. No quantified ROI, savings target, or financial impact discussed.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 18 Feb 2026, immediately following intro call same day. Buyer has remained engaged but evaluation is blocked by parallel ERP selection (Netsuite vs Agriware). Discovery postponed four times (24 Feb, 7 Apr, 7 May, 11 Jun) per Next Step entries tied explicitly to ERP timing. Last buyer email received 24 Jun (5 days ago). Rep reached out 29 Jun and rescheduled catch-up to 28 Jul per Next Step, 2 days before forecasted close. Deal is live and responsive but externally paced by ERP decision. No forward movement on MEDDPICC (EB, champion, budget, demo) since February intro.

champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 18 Feb, Next Step series Mar–Jun
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Mike Bailey is a passive, operational contact with no advocacy, no senior access, and no demonstrated ability to drive the deal. He is responsive but not a driver. Champion development is a critical gap.

items.action: Deliver discovery and demo immediately after ERP decision (target week of 28 Jul catch-up call).
items.trigger: ERP finalists narrowed to Netsuite and Agriware on 22 Jun; catch-up rescheduled to 28 Jul on 29 Jun, 2 days before forecasted close. Discovery postponed four times since February.
items.expected_effect: Converts four months of exploratory holding pattern into active evaluation. Unblocks demo, validates fit, and positions Zycus to differentiate vs Zip. Critical to maintain momentum post-ERP decision and prevent further slip.

items.action: Map and engage the CFO or senior finance sponsor to validate budget and business case before demo.
items.trigger: $120k investment requires executive sign-off. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or budget discussion across five touchpoints. Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager) cannot authorize $120k spend.
items.expected_effect: Establishes executive alignment and budget validation required to close. Prevents late-stage stall when Mike escalates to CFO for approval. CFO on record as account contact but not engaged on this opp.

items.action: Run a Zycus vs Zip competitive positioning session with Mike Bailey and Matt Toso before the demo.
items.trigger: Zip engaged a few weeks prior to 18 Feb intro call. Mike described Zycus and Zip as 'kinda similar maybe.' No differentiation or competitive positioning has occurred.
items.expected_effect: Positions Zycus's full S2P suite (sourcing, spend analysis, Merlin AI intake, organic build) vs Zip's narrower P2P scope as the long-term strategic choice. Makes Zip's limited breadth the liability before demo and evaluation deepen.

items.action: Multi-thread to IT and Finance by bringing Austin Hegwood (Director IT) into the ERP integration and demo discussion.
items.trigger: ERP integration a stated concern on 18 Feb. Austin Hegwood (Director IT) is account contact but not engaged on this opp. Single-thread risk with only Mike Bailey and Matt Toso.
items.expected_effect: Validates ERP integration feasibility (Netsuite or Agriware) and brings technical stakeholder into evaluation. Reduces single-thread risk and strengthens technical win case.

items.action: Quantify the business case with Mike Bailey: target savings, ROI, or efficiency gain from centralizing purchasing and improving visibility.
items.trigger: Operational pain confirmed on 18 Feb (decentralized purchasing, limited visibility, disjointed process) but no financial metric or ROI discussed. Mike downplayed maverick spend and noted cost concern.
items.expected_effect: Converts qualitative pain into quantified value case required for CFO sign-off. Strengthens business case and justifies $120k investment.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer is engaged and responsive. Last email received 24 Jun (5 days ago). Rep rescheduled catch-up 29 Jun to 28 Jul per Next Step. Mike Bailey and Matt Toso attended five scheduled touchpoints (18 Feb, 24 Feb, 7 Apr, 7 May, 11 Jun) even though discovery has not progressed. Buyer has not gone dark or disengaged. However, all postponements are buyer-initiated and tied to ERP timing, signaling Zycus is not a priority until ERP is resolved. Engagement is consistent but passive.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 18 Feb 2026, 131 days old. In Formal Evaluation 131 days. Close date 30 Jul is 31 days away. Discovery postponed four times (24 Feb, 7 Apr, 7 May, 11 Jun) pending ERP finalization. Next catch-up scheduled 28 Jul, 2 days before forecasted close. Moving from discovery to demo, pricing, EB sign-off, legal, and contracting in 31 days is not realistic. Buyer engaged and responsive (last email received 24 Jun, 5 days ago; catch-up rescheduled 29 Jun per Next Step), but the ERP dependency is a hard blocker. Deal is fundamentally healthy but the date is not credible.
evidence: Next Step 29 Jun: catch-up call pushed to 28 Jul, still pending ERP decision (Netsuite vs Agriware).
Last buyer email 24 Jun (5 days ago) confirms active engagement.
Discovery postponed four times since February: 24 Feb (cancelled), 7 Apr (not recorded), 7 May (not recorded), 11 Jun (not recorded). Next Step shows series of reschedules tied to ERP evaluation.
Only 31 days to close. Enterprise P2P deals require 3–4 months post-discovery (demo, shortlist, pricing, EB validation, legal, contracting). This timeline cannot complete by 30 Jul.
Buyer is engaged and the deal is live, but the ERP blocker and compressed timeline make the current close date indefensible.
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy deal blocked by buyer's parallel ERP selection, close date 30 Jul 2026 (31 days out) will slip.

flags: Single-threaded risk: Only Mike Bailey (Logistics Manager) engaged across all touchpoints; no senior stakeholders (CFO, VP Ops, IT Director) mapped or involved. Multi-thread to decision-makers and budget authority immediately.

Discovery stall for four months: Four scheduled discovery calls since 24 Feb, none yielding deep recorded content. The 25 Jun discovery may or may not have occurred (no confirmation logged). Confirm discovery completion, capture detailed requirements, and schedule demo within 7 days or the deal will slip further.
Close date 30 Jul (31 days out) is not credible. Deal has not completed discovery, much less demos, shortlist, vendor selection, pricing, EB sign-off, legal review, or contracting.
Weak champion: Mike Bailey is a coordinator, not a driver. No evidence of advocacy, senior access, or proactive deal progression.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is healthy and engaged but blocked by buyer's ERP decision and missing all senior-level validation. Three high-leverage moves will accelerate post-ERP and position Zycus to win against Zip.

customer_preference.evidence: Mike Bailey described Zycus and Zip as 'kinda similar maybe' on 18 Feb intro call, showing no clear preference. Exploratory tone, no praise or favorable comparison. Buyer is evaluating both but has not expressed a lean.

competitive_position.summary: Zip is the only named competitor, engaged by Mike Bailey and Matt Toso a few weeks prior to 18 Feb intro call. Zip is a direct P2P peer and is in active consideration. Threat level is medium: Zip is live in the buyer's evaluation but no preference signal has been stated. Mike described Zycus and Zip as 'kinda similar maybe,' indicating no clear differentiation has occurred. Additional competition may surface post-ERP decision if the consulting company recommends another solution. Zycus has not yet run a competitive positioning session or delivered a demo to differentiate. The wedge is Zycus's full source-to-pay suite (sourcing, spend analysis, eProcurement, Merlin AI intake) vs Zip's narrower P2P scope, but this has not been activated with the buyer.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin AI intake and autonomous sourcing agent were introduced on 18 Feb intro call. Mike Bailey asked a clarifying question about the AP Smartdesk email-to-PO feature, showing interest. However, no AI-specific business case or positioning depth has been delivered. With fragmented communication and low PO system adoption, the AI intake story is a strong fit, but it has not been activated beyond feature-level mention. AI positioning is under-developed.

items.quote: We have a kind of a crude purchase order system, but doesn't function very efficiently and I don't know. Really, Matt and I are about the only ones using it.
items.criterion: Centralized eProcurement to replace crude, underutilized PO system

items.quote: We're more interested in the sourcing, the spend analysis, and the eProcurement.
items.criterion: Sourcing and spend analysis to identify savings and centralize buying

items.quote: We integrate the ERP so information can be flowing back and forth. So it's not we're not an ERP replacement. We have open APIs and can integrate with any kind of ERP that has that capability.
Communication fragmented across email, Microsoft Teams, and Monday, complicating workflows. No one knows how to communicate anymore.
items.criterion: Conversational AI intake to improve adoption and streamline requests

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Mike Bailey expressed clear interest in eProcurement, sourcing, and spend analysis on 18 Feb, aligned with Zycus's core modules. He ruled out contract management and was uncertain on eInvoicing. Zycus's ability to integrate with any ERP via open APIs directly addresses the buyer's ERP evaluation concern. The fragmented communication and underutilized PO system align well with Merlin intake (Teams/web-based conversational AI) and guided buying workflows. No misalignment or exposure has surfaced. Fit is strong on stated needs, but buyer has not yet seen a demo or validated fit in depth.

• The Feb 24 and Apr 7 meetings were cancelled or not recorded, and the May 7 session is also unrecorded. With only one short qualification call on record and no buyer-voiced AI signal, the 'Important' tag is doing the work here, which is enough for Curious but not enough for confidence.

deltas.subject: Map and engage the CFO or senior finance sponsor before ERP decision concludes. $120k P2P investment requires executive sign-off. deltas.subject: Run a Zycus vs Zip competitive positioning session with Mike Bailey and Matt Toso before the demo. Zip is live in evaluation and no differentiation has occurred. Highlight Zycus's full S2P suite (sourcing, spend analysis, Merlin AI intake, organic build vs point solutions) and use the decentralization pain (five locations, fragmented communication) as the wedge. Make Zip's narrower scope the liability.

deltas.subject: Multi-thread to IT and Finance. value.how_we_win: Differentiate Zycus's full S2P suite (sourcing, spend analysis, Merlin AI intake, organic build) vs Zip's narrower P2P offering; position the AI-driven intake and autonomous sourcing as the long-term value play once ERP is settled. Deliver the demo to showcase depth.

value.name: Zip
value.source: SF Competitors__c
value.how_we_win: Merlin AI agentic procurement automation (intake intelligence, supplier negotiation agent, I2O narrative) plus ML iAnalyze spend visibility and iSource supplier collaboration vs Zip's simpler approval workflows. Make Merlin the Phase 2 centerpiece and run competitive discovery to map Zip's strengths/weaknesses and buyer perception.
